operating_state: Total revenue increased 6% to $58.2 billion driven by 6% capacity expansion and continued strength in domestic and international travel demand, particularly for premium products. The CrowdStrike outage in July caused approximately $380 million in direct revenue impact from 7,000 flight cancellations over five days, reducing expected capacity growth by 0.4 percentage points for the year. SkyMiles cash sales to American Express grew 8% to $7.4 billion, reflecting strong loyalty program performance.
cost_structure: Total operating expenses increased 6% to $52.2 billion, primarily from higher employee-related costs due to wage increases and volume-related expenses from capacity expansion. Non-fuel unit costs increased 2.8% to 13.54 cents per available seat mile. The CrowdStrike disruption generated approximately $170 million in additional operating expenses from customer reimbursements and crew costs, partially offset by $50 million in fuel savings from cancelled flights.
material_events: Delta paid $1.4 billion in profit sharing to employees in February 2024 and will pay another $1.4 billion in February 2025. The company repaid approximately $4.0 billion in debt and finance leases during 2024, including $1.1 billion in early repayments. Christophe Beck was elected to the Board of Directors in December 2024.
macro_exposures: Information technology infrastructure vulnerabilities were elevated following the July 2024 CrowdStrike outage that disrupted Windows-based systems globally, affecting 1.4 million customers through flight delays and cancellations. Climate change risks include increased frequency and severity of weather events potentially causing higher delays, cancellations, and fuel consumption.
forward_outlook: Management held an Investor Day in November 2024 outlining strategic direction and capital allocation plans. The company generated $3.4 billion in free cash flow and maintained $6.1 billion in liquidity at year-end, positioning for continued investment in operations and debt reduction initiatives.
uncertainty_profile: Debt agreements contain financial covenants with minimum coverage ratios that could trigger early amortization events or require additional collateral if breached. Labor relations remain governed by the Railway Labor Act with 20% of workforce unionized, primarily pilots, creating potential for work disruptions if collective bargaining agreements cannot be reached.
